svg.title: SAE EV Bike suspension project — step-by-step action plan
svg.desc: Three phases: immediate fixes this week, SAE deliverables in weeks 1–4, and model upgrades in weeks 3–6

g.text: Phase 1 — Fix the tool before using it (this week)

svg.text: Fix FRF chart to log scale on x-axis + mark body resonance and wheel hop frequency
Remove duplicate setProfile / setMainTab definitions (dead code, lines 537–562)
Add bottoming-out flag: warn when max_suspension_travel exceeds 120 mm (rear limit)
Replace silent FRF error catch with visible error state in the chart panel
Add run overlay: store last 3 results and draw them as faded traces on time + FRF charts
Increase Pareto sweep grid to 20×20 (400 points) and rename label to "Design space sweep"
Baseline characterisation — get real vehicle data (weigh frame, battery, wheels separately)
Run ISO 8608 Class C @ 40 km/h with actual params → produce 1-page table: ISO RMS + comfort band + tyre force
Spring rate decision — run 20×20 Pareto sweep, k range = 0.7×–1.5× baseline, filter bottomed-out points
Output: Pareto plot + CSV export + recommended k–c pair with justification
Speed sweep report — run ISO 8608 Class C at 20 / 40 / 60 / 80 / 100 km/h manually
Plot ISO 2631 vs speed for baseline + recommended tuning. Mark comfort threshold bands (0.315, 0.63, 1.0 m/s²)

g.text: Phase 3 — Model upgrades (weeks 3–6, run in parallel)

svg.text: Split damper into c_compression + c_rebound inputs (backend + two sliders in UI) — ~1 week
Add separate front fork sliders: k_front, c_front (prep for half-car model) — ~1 week
Implement 4-DOF half-car pitch model: add wheelbase L, CoG height h, pitch inertia Iy inputs — 2–3 weeks
Add battery mass input (mass + height from ground) as separate sprung sub-mass — ~1 week
